mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy environment, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and encourages investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity valuations are supported by lower discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates generally support equity valuations
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative on average when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, which typically leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with stable or low inflation indicates healthy consumer demand without immediate pressure for monetary tightening. This environment supports strong corporate revenues and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.3% MoM AND CPIAUCSL growth < 0.3% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS growth is strong and CPIAUCSL growth is contained.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production, particularly when unemployment is low, signifies strong manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, indicating robust economic expansion. This translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output correlates with economic health
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during periods of low unemployment does not lead to positive SPX returns on average over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals a significant weakening in the housing market and broader economic demand, often preceding or accompanying economic downturns. This leads to reduced investment and consumer confidence, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:housing market is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where HOUST falls by more than 10% over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer income and spending. This robust economic activity translates into strong corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative on average when PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation combined with rising federal funds rates indicates a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing price pressures. This environment increases borrowing costs, compresses profit margins, and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3% MoM AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% MoM
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the combined signal of high inflation and rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in real GDP growth signals a robust and expanding economy, which typically translates to higher corporate revenues and profits. This strong fundamental performance drives positive investor sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary indicator of economic health
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods following a quarter where GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth exceeds 3.0%.
